slides.title: Fee variances surface too late
bullets: 3.8% of invoiced fee lines diverge from the contracted tariff
Only visible at the month-end close — leakage compounds for weeks
Three systems report three different variance totals
slides.note: Today the trigger is the close, not the event.

slides.title: Reconciliation is done by hand
bullets: Analyst rebuilds variance in an end-user spreadsheet (EUC)
Finance carries the leakage and the rework
Governance cannot evidence fee accuracy to source

slides.title: Governance says: observe and reconcile
bullets: Maps to DR-1 Data Quality (DR-1.1-003 / DR-1.3-001 / DR-1.4-001)
Controls CTRL-DQ-003 / 006 / 012 take residual risk to Very Low
Commercial data only — no PSU PII; BCBS 239 lineage to source
slides.note: Read-only observability + reconciliation, not a new processing purpose.

slides.title: A fee-variance reconciliation monitor
bullets: Surface open variances per invoice line, not at month-end
One reconciled figure across ledger, invoice, and contract
slides.note: Prototype is direction, not specification — delivery authors the build.
